title: Phonons and magnetism of kagome FeGe probed by nuclear resonant scattering
authors: Yu Tang, Saizheng Cao, Sijie Xu, Xiaokun Teng, Sven Velten, Ilya Sergeev, Pengcheng Dai, Yilin Wang, Yu Song
abstract: Kagome FeGe hosts a $2\times2\times2$ charge-density wave (CDW) that strongly interplays with antiferromagnetic order. Here, we report $^{57}$Fe nuclear resonant scattering measurement to study FeGe across its long-range CDW and incommensurate magnetic transitions. Upon entering the CDW state, hardening of acoustic phonons and optical phonons around 22~meV, 27~meV, and 31~meV are observed in the Fe partial phonon density of states, which can be qualitatively captured by first-principle calculations. Upon entering the incommensurate magnetic phase, neither the phonon density of states nor the hyperfine interaction parameters change significantly, although a subtle feature associated with the incommensurate magnetic order or slow fluctuations is detected in the time-domain Mössbauer spectra. These findings show that the CDW in kagome FeGe significantly modifies its lattice dynamics and magnetism, evidencing an intertwined nature of the spin, charge, and lattice degrees of freedom.
summary: The research investigates the complex interplay between charge density waves and magnetism in kagome FeGe using advanced spectroscopic techniques. By measuring phonon spectra and magnetic hyperfine fields across phase transitions, the authors demonstrate that the formation of a CDW significantly alters the lattice structure while probing subtle differences in coupling strength at subsequent magnetic transitions.
main_problem: To investigate the interplay between charge density wave (CDW) order, antiferromagnetic (AFM) order, and lattice dynamics in kagome FeGe.
main_result: The CDW state significantly modifies the lattice dynamics by causing phonon hardening, while the AFM order is enhanced below $T_{CDW}$. The coupling between CDW and magnetism appears distinct from the behavior observed at the incommensurate magnetic transition ($T_{IC}$).
method: The study employs $^{57}	ext{Fe}$ Nuclear Inelastic Scattering (NIS) to probe phonon density of states, time-domain Mössbauer spectroscopy to measure hyperfine interactions, and is complemented by specific heat and susceptibility measurements.
model_or_system: The physical system is kagome FeGe. The analysis probes the material's behavior across multiple electronic/structural transitions ($T_{CDW}$ and $T_{IC}$) which involve coupled spin, charge, and lattice degrees of freedom.
key_observables: Phonon density of states (PDOS) hardening at specific energies ($\sim 22	ext{ meV}, 27	ext{ meV}, 31	ext{ meV}$); magnetic hyperfine field ($\vec{B}_{hf}$) enhancement; changes in lattice dynamics parameters like the Debye level.
important_parameters: Transition temperatures ($T_{CDW} \approx 115	ext{ K}$, $T_{IC} \approx 65	ext{ K}$); phonon energies (22, 27, 31 meV).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes that the observed changes in lattice dynamics and magnetism are directly coupled to the formation of the CDW state. DFT calculations provide a theoretical baseline for comparison.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the temperature dependence of the Fe partial PDOS showing hardening across $T_{CDW}$, and show time-domain Mössbauer spectra detailing magnetic hyperfine field changes around both transition temperatures.
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes experimental data (NIS, Mössbauer) across different temperature regimes relative to known phase transitions ($T_{CDW}, T_{IC}$). It compares these findings against theoretical DFT calculations to build a comprehensive picture of the coupled order parameters.
why_interesting: This work exemplifies how lattice dynamics measurements (phonons) can be used as highly sensitive probes for subtle electronic instabilities like CDWs, which is relevant in understanding correlated electron behavior beyond simple band theory descriptions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

FIG. 3. (a) The Fe partial PDOS of FeGe g(E), measured at several temperatures. Zoomed-in views of g(E) are shown around (b) ∼22 meV, (c) ∼27 meV, and (d) ∼31 meV. (d) Temperature dependence of g(E)/E2. The solids lines are ﬁts to obtain the Debye level. (f) Temperature dependence of the Debye level.

FIG. 4. (a) Calculated Fe partial PDOS with and without Coulomb interaction U. (b) Comparison of the calculated Fe partial PDOS of FeGe with and without CDW. The dashed boxes highlight energies ranges that are signiﬁcantly by the CDW.

Fig. 4(a) shows the calculated PDOS for FeGe without CDW, showing an overall reasonable agreement with ex- perimental data at RT. DFT underestimates the PDOS above ∼40 meV and overestimates the PDOS around ∼31 meV, which is unaﬀected by the soft force constant and the Coulomb interaction U in the calculations. Such a systematic deviation between DFT and experimental PDOS may result from the presence of signiﬁcant elec- tronic correlations in FeGe, which cannot be captured using single-particle calculations.
